fullArticle:
AI data centre battery storage announcements on both sides of the Atlantic explore the tapping of grid-balancing revenues and distributed computing.

While there appears to be widespread recognition that battery energy storage systems (BESS) can be a valuable tool for data centre developers to meet both energy capacity and computing power needs, the business models are less well established.

As Omri Tayyara, business manager at standards agency CSA Group wrote in an article for PV Tech Power Vol.46, energy storage can alleviate concerns over the available hosting capacity of the electricity grid while serving the increasing power needs of artificial intelligence (AI) training loads, which are far beyond anything seen previously from even the largest of the previous generation of data cent...
